---
title: "lme_f_contrast"
generated: true
description: "Per-voxel F-test of a multi-row mixed-model contrast $C  beta = 0$."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.stats.lme.reml`](../nitrix-stats-lme-reml.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:lme_f_contrast -->
```python
lme_f_contrast(result: 'Union[REMLResult, LMEResult]', contrast: Float[Array, 'L p'], *, dof: ContrastDof = 'satterthwaite', X: Optional[Float[Array, 'N p']] = None, Z: Optional[Float[Array, 'N q']] = None) -> LMEFContrast
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:lme_f_contrast -->
Per-voxel F-test of a multi-row mixed-model contrast $C \beta = 0$.

For an $L$-row contrast matrix $C$ (a single `(p,)` row is
promoted to $L = 1$), the Wald F-statistic is

$$
F = (C \beta)^{\top} (C \operatorname{Cov}(\beta) C^{\top})^{-1}
    (C \beta) / L
$$

with $\operatorname{Cov}(\beta) = (X^{\top} V^{-1} X)^{-1}$ from
`result.fixed_cov`.  The denominator degrees of freedom use the
**Fai-Cornelius / lmerTest** multivariate-Satterthwaite construction
(`dof='satterthwaite'`, the default):

1. eigendecompose
   $M = C \operatorname{Cov}(\beta) C^{\top}
   = P \operatorname{diag}(d) P^{\top}$ (a small $L \times L$
   symmetric solve, cuSOLVER-free `sym_eig_jacobi`);
2. each eigendirection $l_m = (P^{\top} C)_m$ is an independent 1-df
   contrast with variance $d_m$ and a per-direction Satterthwaite
   $\nu_m = 2 d_m^2 / (g_m^{\top} \operatorname{Cov}(\theta)\, g_m)$,
   $g_{m,k} = w_m^{\top} M_k w_m$,
   $w_m = \operatorname{Cov}(\beta)\, l_m$ (the same $M_k$ /
   $\operatorname{Cov}(\theta)$ the fit formed, `result.grad_m` /
   `result.theta_cov`);
3. combine by the E-method:
   $E = \sum_m \nu_m / (\nu_m - 2)$ over $\nu_m > 2$, and
   $\mathrm{df}_2 = 2 E / (E - L)$.

For $L = 1$ this collapses **exactly** to [`lme_t_contrast`](../nitrix-stats-lme-reml/lme_t_contrast.qmd)
($F = t^2$, `df2` the t-Satterthwaite df, same p-value).  The
F-statistic is differentiable; the denominator df is not (the iterative
eigensolver feeds it under `stop_gradient` -- a model-selection-style
scalar).

`dof='kr'` instead applies the **Kenward-Roger** small-sample correction:
the adjusted covariance $\Phi_A$ and the moment-matched
scaled-$F$, referred to $F(L, m)$.  It rebuilds the rotated
basis, so re-pass the original design as `X=` / `Z=` (the compressed
[`REMLResult`](../nitrix-stats-lme-reml/REMLResult.qmd) does not retain it); $F = t^2$ consistency with
`lme_t_contrast(dof='kr')` holds for $L = 1$.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:lme_f_contrast -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `result` | `'Union[REMLResult, LMEResult]'` | — | — | A [`REMLResult`](../nitrix-stats-lme-reml/REMLResult.qmd) ([`reml_fit`](../nitrix-stats-lme-reml/reml_fit.qmd), the scalar-intercept tier) **or** an [`LMEResult`](../nitrix-stats-lme-reml/LMEResult.qmd) ([`lme_fit`](../nitrix-stats-lme-reml/lme_fit.qmd) with a random slope) -- both carry the `fixed_cov` / `theta_cov` / `grad_m` inference fields. `dof='kr'` accepts only a [`REMLResult`](../nitrix-stats-lme-reml/REMLResult.qmd). |
| `contrast` | `Float` | `L` × `p` | — | `(L, p)` contrast matrix (or a `(p,)` single-row contrast). |
| `dof` | `ContrastDof` | — | `'satterthwaite'` | `'satterthwaite'` (default) or `'kr'` (Kenward-Roger; pass `X` / `Z`). |
| `X` | `Optional[Float[Array, 'N p']]` | — | `None` | The original fixed `(N, p)` / random `(N, q)` design -- required for `dof='kr'`. |
| `Z` | `Optional[Float[Array, 'N q']]` | — | `None` | The original fixed `(N, p)` / random `(N, q)` design -- required for `dof='kr'`. |

**Returns** `LMEFContrast`. The per-voxel F-contrast record [`LMEFContrast`](../nitrix-stats-lme-reml/LMEFContrast.qmd) `(f, df1, df2, p_value)`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:lme_f_contrast -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `L` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
